prompts.label: First Git repository
prompts.text:
I'm new to Git. Walk me through my first local repository.

- git init, create README.md, git add, git commit
- git status before and after each step explained
- What is .git folder in one sentence

Assume Windows terminal; mention git --version check.

prompts.label: Clone a GitHub repo
prompts.text:
Explain git clone for a beginner.

- Clone a generic hello-repo URL pattern (placeholder)
- What files appear locally
- How to open the project in Cursor/VS Code
- Difference clone vs download ZIP

Do not use a real private repo; use public example style.

prompts.label: Create and switch branch
prompts.text:
Teach Git branches for beginners.

- git branch, git switch (or checkout) to feature/add-footer
- Make a small commit on the branch
- git log --oneline --graph with 2–3 commits described

Explain why branches exist on a team.

prompts.label: Merge branch into main
prompts.text:
Show merging a feature branch into main.

- Steps: switch to main, git merge feature/...
- Explain fast-forward vs merge commit simply
- What if both branches edited the same line? (introduce conflict concept)

Use a toy example file app.txt.

prompts.label: Resolve a merge conflict
prompts.text:
Simulate a merge conflict and how to fix it.

- Show conflict markers in a file
- Steps: open file, edit to final text, git add, git commit
- How to abort merge with git merge --abort

Keep calm tone for beginners who panic at conflicts.

prompts.label: push and pull with origin
prompts.text:
Explain git push and git pull for GitHub beginners.

- Set upstream: git push -u origin main
- Someone else pushed: git pull before you push
- What is origin?

Mention authentication (HTTPS token or SSH) at high level without secrets.

prompts.label: git stash save work
prompts.text:
When should a beginner use git stash?

- Scenario: unfinished changes but must switch branch
- git stash push -m message, git switch, later git stash pop
- Warn about stash pop conflicts briefly

Short exercise steps.

prompts.label: .gitignore essentials
prompts.text:
Create a useful .gitignore for a JavaScript + Python learning folder.

- node_modules/, __pycache__/, .env, dist/, .venv/
- Explain why secrets must not be committed
- What if I already committed .env by mistake? (git rm --cached high level)

Provide full .gitignore file content.

prompts.label: git log and git diff
prompts.text:
Teach reading history with git log and git diff.

- git log --oneline -5
- git diff before commit
- git diff main..feature/branch name

Explain what + and - lines mean in diff output.

prompts.label: Pull request workflow
prompts.text:
Explain the GitHub Pull Request workflow for a junior developer.

- Steps from feature branch to merged main
- What to write in PR description (checklist example)
- Code review etiquette in 4 bullets

No need for GitHub CLI; web UI is fine.

prompts.label: git bisect find bad commit
prompts.text:
My main branch broke last week. Walk me through git bisect start, good, bad, run a test command, bisect reset.
Explain the binary search intuition for beginners.
Show how to bisect with a script exit code.
Mention bisect skip for flaky tests cautiously.
Give a safety tip about detached HEAD confusion.

prompts.label: git worktree parallel checkouts
prompts.text:
Teach git worktree add ../hotfix hotfix-branch for parallel folders.
Explain when this beats stashing for quick reviews.
Show listing and pruning worktrees.
Warn about uncommitted changes conflicts between worktrees.
Relate to CI clone depth only tangentially.

prompts.label: Cherry-pick with conflict resolution
prompts.text:
Explain cherry-pick use case: backport a bugfix commit to a release branch.
Walk through conflict markers, add, cherry-pick --continue.
Contrast with merge and rebase mentally.
Note empty commits and --mainline for merges briefly as vocabulary.
Advise recording the original SHA in the PR description.

prompts.label: Safe undo: revert vs reset rules
prompts.text:
Clarify when git revert is preferred over reset --hard on shared branches.
Show revert -m for merge commits at a conceptual level.
Explain reflog as a local safety net with limits.
Provide a decision flowchart in words for beginners panicking after a bad push.
Emphasize communication with teammates after history repair.

prompts.label: Interactive add patch hunks
prompts.text:
Teach staging part of a file with git add -p / --patch.
Walk through y/n/s/e choices intuitively.
Relate to crafting atomic commits readable in review.
Show split hunk tips at a high level.
Mention GUI alternatives without endorsing a specific tool.

prompts.label: Annotated tags for releases
prompts.text:
Compare lightweight vs annotated tags for semver releases.
Show git tag -a v1.2.0 -m "..." and pushing tags.
Explain how tags differ from branches mentally.
Note signed tags concept as optional advanced reading.
Describe releasing from a tag in CI in one sentence.

prompts.label: Hooks outline without breaking flows
prompts.text:
Outline what a pre-commit hook can validate (format, lint, secrets scan).
Explain local vs CI enforcement complement.
Warn about slow hooks frustrating teams.
Show a pseudo-hook script structure without vendor lock-in.
Discuss bypass policies and when they are acceptable.

prompts.label: Submodules mental model warning
prompts.text:
Introduce submodules as separate repos pinned to a commit.
List three pain points beginners hit (forgot init, detached state, PR noise).
Suggest alternatives like monorepo or package managers when appropriate.
Give minimal clone --recurse-submodules workflow.
Keep depth conceptual; no flame war.

prompts.label: Blame and log story arc
prompts.text:
Teach git blame -L for narrowing lines and ignoring whitespace option idea.
Pair with git log -S for finding when a string disappeared.
Explain how to keep investigations blameless in team culture.
Show linking to issue trackers in commit messages habit.
Finish with a note about moved code limitations.

prompts.label: Pull with rebase vs merge defaults
prompts.text:
Compare git pull --rebase vs merge for feature branch updates.
Explain diverged history diagrams in words.
Describe autostash interaction with rebase pulls.
Give team policy guidance for beginners joining a repo.
Mention configurable pull.rebase safe advice.
